message:
fix(ma): match numpy.ma fix_invalid / ediff1d semantics more precisely

Two separate corrections in crates/fnp-python/src/lib.rs to tighten
parity with numpy.ma:

- fix_invalid: the `fill_value=F` kwarg in numpy.ma.fix_invalid is a
  REPLACEMENT value applied to invalid entries of `.data`, it is NOT
  copied onto the output MaskedArray's `.fill_value` attribute. The
  previous implementation over-propagated `resolved_fill_value` onto
  the output whenever the caller supplied `fill_value=`, even when the
  input was a plain ndarray; upstream numpy leaves the output's
  fill_value at the dtype default in that shape. The condition narrows
  to `input_is_masked` so only the input-inherited path sets
  fill_value explicitly; a small comment captures the invariant so
  future edits don't re-broaden the trigger.

- ma_ediff1d: the previous body was a straight passthrough to
  numpy.ma.ediff1d. It is rewritten to run the diff in-Rust via
  `MaskedArray::ediff1d` / `.take(right)` and `.take(left)` on the
  ravelled input, with a numpy.ma.ediff1d fallback retained behind a
  closure for the cases where numeric extraction fails. The fast path
  preserves masks across the diff, handles <2-element inputs
  explicitly (uses the MaskedArray::ediff1d primitive), and otherwise
  computes right.take(1..) and left.take(0..n-1) to derive the
  first-difference values. to_begin / to_end prefix/suffix prepending
  behaviour is still delegated to the numpy fallback when those kwargs
  are present and structural complexity makes the in-Rust path not
  worth the code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
